page: Microsoft Azure L96s_v4 is a high-capacity server belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 dedicated virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The server is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 23,038 GB of local storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high data throughput. The server achieved top-tier status in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 524.75 GB/sec, Geekbench multi-core scoring at 21,790.0, and PassMark CPU performance. Static web serving throughput also reached the top tier at 113.25 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests showed strong performance, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 6,198.6 GB/sec and uncached large block transfers at 222.04 GB/sec. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, remains in the average tier.

Given its massive 23,038 GB local storage capacity and 768.0 GB of RAM, this server is highly cost-efficient for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The combination of high-speed Intel Xeon processing and extensive local storage makes it suitable for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. It also demonstrates strong capability in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models.
description: The Microsoft Azure L96s_v4 is a storage-optimized and memory-optimized server from the Lsv4 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores), 768.0 GB of RAM, and an extensive 23,038 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, notably in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and static web serving throughput. It also shows strong capabilities in database operations, memory bandwidth, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing. Lacking a GPU, this server offers a highly resource-dense profile for large-scale database management, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L96s_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 23,038 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L96s_v4 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 23,038 GB local storage for high-throughput database and storage workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, storage-dense server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and extensive local storage for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor.
768.0 GB of system memory paired with 23,038 GB of local storage.
Top-tier multi-core performance in decompression and web serving benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and heavy multi-threaded workloads.
